preamble: The realization of a glottal stop /ʔ/ as a nasal consonant /n/, typically occurring in the context of a following syllabic nasal or through the nasalization of the glottal closure.

examples.note: The glottal stop is released directly into the nasal /n/.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Johnson, K. (2014). A Course in Phonetics.
